name: GlyNAC improved redox, mitochondrial, and physical-function endpoints in older adults
summary: A small older-adult RCT supplies the strongest direct human functional signal for the NAD and redox restoration baseline.
statement: In a small randomized trial, 16 weeks of GlyNAC in older adults improved or corrected glutathione deficiency, oxidative stress, mitochondrial dysfunction, inflammatory and endothelial markers, physical function, strength, exercise capacity, and several aging-hallmark biomarkers compared with placebo.
population_or_model: Older adults aged 61 to 80 years, with a young-adult reference group
quantitative_note: The older-adult randomized comparison included 12 GlyNAC and 12 placebo participants; 12 young adults served as a reference group.
caveats: The randomized older-adult sample was small.
The endpoint set was broad and requires independent replication with prespecified primary functional outcomes.
The intervention is a nutrient-precursor combination and should not be generalized to all redox or NAD interventions.
